data.title: Capgemini (ENXTPA:CAP) Margin Slippage Challenges Bullish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Capgemini (ENXTPA:CAP) has reported its FY 2025 results with second half revenue of €11.4b and basic EPS of €5.20, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of €22.5b and EPS of €9.45 that investors will be weighing against recent margin trends. Over the past three reported halves, revenue has moved from €11.0b in H2 2024 to €11.1b in H1 2025 and €11.4b in H2 2025. Basic EPS has shifted from €4.93 to €4.26 and then €5.20, setting up a picture where modest revenue progression meets earnings that are being closely watched against a net profit margin of 7.1% versus 7.6% a year earlier.

See our full analysis for Capgemini.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely shared narratives around Capgemini, highlighting where the data backs the story and where it starts to push back.

See what the community is saying about Capgemini

Advertisement

7.1% net margin now sits below last year’s 7.6%

  • Over the last 12 months, Capgemini converted €22.5b of revenue into €1.6b of net income, for a 7.1% net profit margin compared with 7.6% a year earlier, while trailing EPS went from €9.82 to €9.45.
  • Consensus narrative describes a shift toward higher value cloud, data and AI work as a potential support for future margin resilience. However, the current 7.1% margin and slightly lower trailing EPS indicate that near-term pricing pressure and restructuring costs are still visible in the reported numbers.
    • Analysts are assuming margins eventually reach 8.2%, which would be a step up from the 7.1% reported over the last year.
    • The group’s focus on automation and higher margin digital services is intended to offset current margin pressure, but the latest figures show that effect is not yet reflected in the trailing margin.

TTM revenue at €22.5b, forecast growth of 4.7% a year

  • On a trailing basis, revenue is €22.5b and is described as having grown about 4.7% per year over the last 12 months, which sits below the referenced French market rate of 5.7% a year.
  • Bulls argue that stronger demand for cloud, data and AI work, together with higher growth in regions like Asia Pacific and Latin America, can support faster top line progress. The recent 4.7% revenue growth rate and guidance that acknowledges softer conditions in parts of Europe indicate that any acceleration still has to come through in the reported revenue trend.
    • The bullish view expects revenue to climb faster over time, but the latest period still shows relatively modest growth compared with the wider French market.
    • With manufacturing, automotive and some European markets described as soft, the current growth profile leaves less room for error if those weaker areas do not improve.
Have a look at how bullish investors are framing that potential, and where they think growth could surprise, in 🐂 Capgemini Bull Case.

P/E of 11x, well below DCF fair value estimate

  • At a share price of €104.85, Capgemini trades on a P/E of 11x, with analysis indicating this sits below a DCF fair value of about €212.03 and below an analyst price target of €169.67, while still being slightly higher than the cited peer P/E of 10.3x and lower than the wider European IT average of 18.5x.
  • Bears caution that slower revenue growth and modest margin compression could justify a lower multiple, and they point to recent margin softness and below market revenue growth as backing for that stance, even though the current 11x P/E is materially beneath both the DCF fair value indication and the €169.67 analyst target.
    • The small gap between Capgemini’s 11x P/E and the 10.3x peer average is consistent with concerns about margins and growth running a little behind some peers.
    • At the same time, the large difference between the current price and both the DCF fair value and analyst target shows why many investors still see room for upside if growth and margins track closer to expectations.
If you are weighing that more cautious angle, it is worth seeing how skeptics frame the risks in 🐻 Capgemini Bear Case.

data.title: Towa Pharmaceutical (TSE:4553) Margin Firmness Reinforces Defensive Earnings Narrative In Q3 2026
data.description:

Earnings over the last year grew 11.2% and margins edged up as net profit margin reached 7.9%.   • Over the last 12 months, Towa Pharmaceutical generated ¥269,922 million in revenue and ¥21,444 million in net income, with earnings up 11.2% year over year and net profit margin at 7.9% compared with 7.6% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is how this mix of growth and efficiency lines up with the story of a defensive healthcare business, with:
    • Five year annualized earnings growth of 8.3% sitting alongside that 11.2% one year earnings increase, which heavily supports the idea of a business that has been able to grow profits over time.
    • The 7.9% trailing margin, slightly above the prior 7.6%, suggesting profitability has held up even as trailing twelve month revenue moved from ¥243,942 million to ¥269,922 million in the data provided.

data.title: Assessing NH Foods (TSE:2282) Valuation After Upgraded Guidance And Leadership Change
data.description:
NH Foods (TSE:2282) is in focus after raising its full year earnings and dividend forecasts, and announcing a leadership reshuffle that will see long serving executive Fumio Maeda become President and CEO.

See our latest analysis for NH Foods.

Those guidance upgrades and boardroom changes come after a solid run, with a 1 year total shareholder return of 59.38% and a 3 year total shareholder return of 103.21%. Shorter term share price returns suggest momentum has been steadier rather than rapid.

On our data, NH Foods is trading on a P/E of 21.4x, which screens as expensive both against its own fair P/E estimate and against food sector peers.

For a mature food producer like NH Foods, this usually reflects expectations around steady earnings growth, the resilience of its brands, and the reliability of its dividend stream.

Here, the 21.4x P/E sits above the estimated fair P/E of 20.9x. This suggests the market is pricing NH Foods at a premium to the level our fair ratio points to as a potential anchor. That premium comes on top of earnings that are forecast to grow 8.59% per year, with revenue growth forecasts of 1.5% per year that are slower than both the wider JP market and the company’s recent 11.9% earnings growth.

The comparison with peers is also clear. The JP Food industry average P/E sits at 17.8x and NH Foods' peer group averages 19.6x, so its 21.4x multiple stands meaningfully higher than both. That kind of gap usually implies investors are willing to pay up versus the sector for the company’s current earnings profile, even though return on equity is 5.9% and forecast to remain low at 7.2% in three years.

data.title: Kajima Corporation Just Beat Analyst Forecasts, And Analysts Have Been Updating Their Predictions
data.description:
A week ago, Kajima Corporation (TSE:1812) came out with a strong set of quarterly numbers that could potentially lead to a re-rate of the stock. The company beat forecasts, with revenue of JP¥773b, some 2.8% above estimates, and statutory earnings per share (EPS) coming in at JP¥96.29, 31% ahead of expectations. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. We've gathered the most recent statutory forecasts to see whether the analysts have changed their earnings models, following these results.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Taking into account the latest results, Kajima's seven analysts currently expect revenues in 2027 to be JP¥3.03t, approximately in line with the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are expected to shrink 3.4% to JP¥360 in the same period. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥3.04t and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥363 in 2027. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

See our latest analysis for Kajima

The consensus price target rose 5.0% to JP¥6,593despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of Kajima's earnings by assigning a price premium. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. The most optimistic Kajima analyst has a price target of JP¥9,900 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at JP¥3,300. As you can see the range of estimates is wide, with the lowest valuation coming in at less than half the most bullish estimate, suggesting there are some strongly diverging views on how analysts think this business will perform. As a result it might not be a great idea to make decisions based on the consensus price target, which is after all just an average of this wide range of estimates.

Another way we can view these estimates is in the context of the bigger picture, such as how the forecasts stack up against past performance, and whether forecasts are more or less bullish relative to other companies in the industry. We would highlight that Kajima's revenue growth is expected to slow, with the forecast 0.09% annualised growth rate until the end of 2027 being well below the historical 9.9% p.a. growth over the last five years. Compare this against other companies (with analyst forecasts) in the industry, which are in aggregate expected to see revenue growth of 3.5% annually. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it seems obvious that Kajima is also expected to grow slower than other industry participants.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that there's been no major change in sentiment, with the analysts reconfirming that the business is performing in line with their previous earnings per share estimates. On the plus side, there were no major changes to revenue estimates; although forecasts imply they will perform worse than the wider industry. We note an upgrade to the price target, suggesting that the analysts believes the intrinsic value of the business is likely to improve over time.

With that in mind, we wouldn't be too quick to come to a conclusion on Kajima. Long-term earnings power is much more important than next year's profits. We have forecasts for Kajima going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

You still need to take note of risks, for example - Kajima has 1 warning sign we think you should be aware of.

The company has seen quarterly revenue range from ¥81.0 billion in Q1 2026 to ¥134.1 billion in Q3 2026, alongside EPS swinging between a ¥15.89 loss and a ¥53.26 profit across recent periods.   • What stands out for a more bullish view is that the trailing 12 month revenue of ¥441.9b is growing at about 5.8% per year, yet that same period now shows a ¥13.6b loss, which presses on the idea that revenue growth alone can carry the story.

data.title: Alamos Gold Expansion Study Recasts Island Gold District Growth Outlook
data.description:
• Alamos Gold (TSX:AGI) released its Island Gold District Expansion Study, outlining a 30% increase in mineral reserves.
  • The study details plans to expand processing capacity at the Magino mill to support higher gold output.
  • The update builds on recent drilling results that point to ongoing resource conversion potential in the district.

For investors watching Alamos Gold at CA$61.61, the Island Gold study adds fresh context to a share price that has moved up 12.3% over the past week and 16.9% year to date. The stock is also up 95.8% over the past year and has delivered a very large gain over five years, which puts extra attention on how this expansion plan might influence future operations.

The expansion study focuses on lifting gold production and using the Magino mill as a larger processing hub for the district. Ongoing exploration, along with the 30% reserve increase, gives investors more detail on how Alamos Gold is planning around longer term mine life and potential output from Island Gold.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Alamos Gold by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Alamos Gold.

We've flagged 0 risks for Alamos Gold. See which could impact your investment.

The Island Gold District Expansion Study points to Alamos Gold leaning harder into a hub-and-spoke model around the Magino mill, with high grade ore from Island Gold and nearby deposits feeding a larger processing center. For you as an investor, the 30% reserve uplift and planned higher throughput tie directly into the company’s multi year production guidance, which outlines a path from 570,000 to 650,000 ounces in 2026 to as much as 875,000 ounces in 2028. This scale aims to put Alamos in the same conversation as larger Canadian producers such as Agnico Eagle and Yamana’s successor entities, while still competing with mid tier names such as B2Gold on growth through organic projects.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Alamos Gold Narrative

  • The expansion study supports the existing narrative that higher output from Island Gold and Magino can underpin stronger cash flow and earnings, by converting exploration success into mineable reserves and feeding a larger, more efficient mill.
  • The plan also increases execution risk, since the narrative already highlights dependence on successful expansion and integration of Island Gold and Magino, and the updated production path concentrates even more of the story on these projects performing as planned.
  • The push toward one of Canada’s largest gold operations and potential future acquisitions discussed at Investor Day adds a scale and M&A angle that is not fully reflected in the earlier focus on organic growth and cost synergies alone.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Alamos Gold to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ A larger, multi asset district centered on Magino increases complexity, so delays, cost pressures or technical issues at the shaft and mill could affect the production ranges set out for 2026 to 2028.
  • ⚠️ The expansion is still highly sensitive to gold prices, energy costs, labor availability and permitting or environmental constraints across Canada and Mexico, all of which the company itself lists as key uncertainties.
  • 🎁 The 30% reserve increase, plus years of drilling at Island Gold, Magino, Cline Pick and Edwards, supports the company’s aim to sustain higher throughput and provide better long term visibility on ore sources near existing infrastructure.
  • 🎁 Management’s willingness to pursue acquisitions, but only on a tight value focused shortlist, gives optionality to add further growth projects without relying solely on any single mine.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth keeping an eye on how actual mine development and shaft construction at Island Gold track against the 2026 to 2028 production ranges, and whether the Magino mill upgrade stays on schedule and on budget. The conversion of additional resources to reserves in the district, especially around Cline Pick and Edwards, will also matter for the long term feed profile into Magino. On the corporate side, watch for any M&A moves that fit the value focused criteria discussed at Investor Day, and how those might shift capital allocation between exploration, expansion and potential buybacks.

data.title: Is AGNC Investment (AGNC) Attractively Priced After Strong One Year Share Price Performance?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether AGNC Investment's share price really reflects its underlying value, you are not alone. Many income focused investors keep a close eye on this mortgage REIT.
  • The stock last closed at US$11.36, with returns of 3.9% year to date and 26.0% over 1 year. Shorter term moves have been softer, with a 0.7% decline over 7 days and a 1.8% decline over 30 days.
  • Recent headlines around AGNC have focused on its role as a mortgage REIT in a changing interest rate backdrop, including commentary on how its portfolio positioning and leverage policy respond to shifts in bond markets. These themes help frame why the share price has seen both longer term gains and shorter term pullbacks as investors reassess risk and income prospects.
  • On our checks, AGNC scores a 5 out of 6 valuation rating. This suggests it screens as undervalued across most of our metrics. Next we will walk through the key valuation approaches before finishing with a broader framework that can help you judge its value more clearly.

AGNC Investment delivered 26.0% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Mortgage REITs industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: AGNC Investment Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company is expected to generate above the return that equity investors require, then capitalizes those surplus profits into an intrinsic value per share.

For AGNC Investment, the starting point is its book value of $9.41 per share and a stable earnings figure of $1.51 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 4 analysts. The average Return on Equity is 15.67%, compared with a cost of equity of $0.93 per share. That gap is the excess return, estimated at $0.59 per share.

The model also uses a stable book value of $9.66 per share, again drawn from weighted future Book Value estimates from 4 analysts. Together, these inputs are used to project the stream of future excess returns and convert them into an estimated intrinsic value of about $19.10 per share.

Against the recent share price of $11.36, this Excess Returns valuation implies the stock is 40.5% undervalued. This suggests the market is pricing AGNC below the value indicated by its expected return on equity.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests AGNC Investment is undervalued by 40.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for AGNC Investment.

Approach 2: AGNC Investment Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like AGNC Investment, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It gives you a quick sense of how the market values those earnings relative to other options you could invest in.

What counts as a normal or fair P/E usually reflects two broad ideas: how much growth investors expect from those earnings, and how much risk they see in the business. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk often lines up with a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk can support a lower multiple.

AGNC currently trades on a P/E of 8.34x. That sits below the Mortgage REITs industry average P/E of 12.45x and the broader peer average of 17.55x. Simply Wall St also calculates a Fair Ratio for AGNC of 13.62x. This is a proprietary view of what the P/E could be based on factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risk profile, rather than just a straight comparison with peers.

Because the Fair Ratio of 13.62x is higher than the current P/E of 8.34x, AGNC screens as undervalued on this P/E based approach.

Result: UNDERVALUED

  • Analysts' consensus view expects TOPPAN to lean more on higher margin areas like sustainable packaging and digital security. However, the current 4.2% trailing margin, below last year's 5.2%, shows that portfolio shifts and cost measures are still working through a business where some legacy print activities and regulation sensitive packaging segments can weigh on profitability.

One off gain of ¥69.6b clouds earnings quality

  • Trailing 12 month earnings include a ¥69.6b one off gain, which is large compared with the ¥75.2b of net income excluding extra items, so reported profit over that period is heavily affected by this non recurring item.
  • Critics highlight that transformation costs, M&A spend and weaker electronics demand could strain returns, and the size of this ¥69.6b one off gain relative to underlying earnings makes it harder to judge how far structural reforms, portfolio exits and acquisitions like the Sonoco packaging deal are improving steady state profitability versus simply lifting reported EPS in the short run.

Skeptical investors are watching how much of TOPPAN's story rests on repeatable cash generation rather than accounting gains and timing effects, and the detailed bear case for the stock digs into those concerns more directly. 🐻 TOPPAN Holdings Bear Case

Forecast 7.8% earnings growth vs 2.9% revenue

  • Forward looking estimates point to earnings growth of 7.8% per year compared with revenue growth of 2.9% per year. The trailing P/E of 18.4x at a share price of ¥4,890 sits above the 14.5x industry average but below a 21.6x peer average, and alongside a DCF fair value of ¥5,451.84.
  • Supporters argue that expansion into sustainable packaging, digital security and advanced semiconductors can support this faster earnings trajectory by shifting the mix toward businesses where margins are expected to be stronger. They also point to the gap between the current price of ¥4,890 and both the ¥5,451.84 DCF fair value and a ¥5,975.00 analyst price target as evidence the market may not yet be fully pricing in those segments, even though recent trailing margins and the drag from older print and electronics activities keep the bullish case reliant on execution over the next few years.

data.description:

Over the past few quarters the company has seen quarterly revenue move from ¥47.0b in Q1 FY 2025 to ¥53.4b in Q4, while net income ranged from ¥3.3b to ¥9.2b and quarterly EPS stepped from ¥13.23 to ¥37.14. 104.8% earnings jump meets slower 3.6% revenue trend

  • Over the last 12 months, Mabuchi Motor’s net income of ¥26,272 million and revenue of ¥200,417 million translate into earnings growth of 104.8%, while trailing revenue is described as growing at about 3.6% a year.
  • What stands out for the bullish view is that a more than 2x earnings increase sits on top of only modest revenue growth of about 3.6% a year. This suggests:
    • Profitability did a lot of the heavy lifting, with net profit margin moving to 13.1% from 6.5% over the same period.
    • Quarterly net income in FY 2025 stayed in positive territory each quarter, between ¥3,325 million and ¥9,154 million, after a loss of ¥1,442 million in Q3 FY 2024.

Margins at 13.1% while earnings forecast to fall 6.9% a year

  • Trailing net profit margin is 13.1%, compared with 6.5% a year earlier, yet earnings are forecast to decline by about 6.9% a year on average over the next three years.
  • Bears focus on that 6.9% annual earnings decline forecast even with stronger recent profitability, arguing the current margin may be tough to maintain. The numbers show:
    • Recent quarters already show some variation, with FY 2025 quarterly net income ranging from ¥3,325 million in Q1 to ¥9,154 million in Q3.
    • Revenue is expected to grow at around 3.6% per year, which sits below the 5% per year JP market benchmark, so top line growth is not flagged as a strong support for future earnings.

P/E of 15x and ¥788.31 DCF fair value send mixed valuation signals

  • At a share price of ¥1,610.5, Mabuchi Motor trades on a P/E of 15x, slightly below the JP Electrical industry average of 15.3x and well below the peer average of 29x, while a DCF fair value of ¥788.31 sits materially below the current price.
  • For a cautious, valuation driven view, the contrast between relative and absolute metrics is key, because:

    • The DCF fair value of ¥788.31, which is roughly half of the ¥1,610.5 share price, points to a much more conservative view of future cash flows than what the trailing P/E alone might imply.
